hypotheses.name: SV4/SV5 PPI co-pole test
hypotheses.method: SVD of 209-gene mean-centered embeddings at each of 12 layers. For SV4 (index 3) and SV5 (index 4): compute co-pole rate (K=52 top/bottom) for 3092 STRING pairs (score>=0.4). Compare to gene-label shuffle null (N=300). Report z-score and emp_p per layer.
hypotheses.result_value: SV4 mean_z=2.21, sig=7/12; SV5 mean_z=1.65, sig=6/12
hypotheses.next_action: SV4 shows meaningful PPI co-pole geometry (z=5.16 at L0, prominent early layers). Profile SV4's GO biology (analogous to H01 in iter_0014 for SV3). SV5 intermittent (L3-L4, L8-L10); less robust.

hypotheses.name: STRING confidence gradient: co-pole z vs score quintile
hypotheses.method: Split 3092 STRING pairs into 5 quintiles by score (edges: [0.40,0.46), [0.46,0.53), [0.53,0.64), [0.64,0.80), [0.80,1.0]). For each quintile, compute mean SV2 co-pole z-score across 12 layers (K=52, N_null=200). Compute Spearman correlation between quintile midpoint score and mean z.
hypotheses.next_action: Near-perfect monotonic gradient: interaction confidence directly predicts geometric co-localization strength. This is a key quantitative claim. Replicate with SV3 axis and report in paper. Also test at individual layers for layer-specificity.

hypotheses.name: GO co-annotation vs PPI as driver of SV2 geometry
hypotheses.method: Compute GO Jaccard similarity for all 3092 STRING pairs. Median-split by GO Jaccard (thresh=0.042) and STRING score (thresh=0.585). Define 4 groups: PPI-only (high string, low GO), GO-only (low string, high GO), Both, Neither. Compute SV2 co-pole z-score per group at each of 12 layers (K=52, N_null=300).
hypotheses.primary_metric: mean z-score per group across 12 layers
hypotheses.result_value: Both: z=5.27 (12/12 sig); PPI-only: z=2.66 (12/12 sig); GO-only: z=1.89 (7/12 sig); Neither: z=0.70 (0/12 sig)
hypotheses.next_action: PPI-only signal (z=2.66, 12/12 sig) confirms physical interaction drives geometry independent of functional annotation. GO-only signal (z=1.89, 7/12 sig) suggests GO co-annotation also contributes but is weaker. 'Both' synergy (z=5.27) suggests the two signals are partially additive. Reduce GO Jaccard threshold to top/bottom quintile for cleaner separation.
